items.hook: At least 120 people are killed and 130 injured by a suicide bombing in Diyala Governorate, Iraq.
items.body: A suicide car bombing occurred on 17 July 2015 in the Iraqi city of Khan Bani Saad, targeting a local marketplace. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: ‘From the father of the guy who made Obsession’: is the nepo dad the new nepo baby?
items.body:

items.body:

items.hook: Ava DuVernay to make Netflix documentary 14th on birthright citizenship
items.body:
The film-maker behind Selma and 13th will focus on the 14th amendment for a new film out later this year as Donald Trump targets those protected by it

items.body: The Runner by Scarlett Thomas; The Madman by Henning Mankell; Everything She Didn’t Say by Jane Casey; The Spy and the Snake by MJ Robotham; Murder at the End of the World by Akane Araki

items.hook: ‘A masterpiece in every way’: The Odyssey earns near universal acclaim as full reviews published
items.body:
Christopher Nolan’s epic was given five stars by the Guardian, Independent and Telegraph, while the New York Times called it a ‘monumental adaptation’

items.hook: Cabbage stew is a stew prepared using cabbage as a primary ingredient.
items.body: Basic preparations of the dish use cabbage, various vegetables such as onion, carrot and celery, and vegetable stock. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: Jackass: Best and Last review – kings of gross-out comedy’s final, funny farewell
items.body:
So-called final outing for Johnny Knoxville and his daring, stunt-hungry pals might be close to a greatest hits reel, but there are enough laughs to warrant the nostalgia

items.hook: The largest number of United States midshipman casualties in a single event results from an aircraft crash in Florida, killing 44.
items.body: A midshipman is an officer of the lowest rank in the Royal Navy, United States Navy, and many Commonwealth navies. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Natalya Varley is a Soviet and Russian film and theater actress, who became famous in 1966 for her part in the comedy Kidnapping, Caucasian Style.
items.body: In 1989 she was designated as a Meritorious Artist of RSFSR. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Paranirupasingam Devakumaran also spelt as Thevakumar was a minority Sri Lankan Tamil Television journalist who reported for the Maharaja Television which operated MTV English, Sirasa TV in Sinhala and Sakthi TV in Tamil in Jaffna.
items.body: He and his friend Mahendran Varadan were hacked to death in Navanthurai near Jaffna on 28 May 2008 while he was on his way home by the paramilitary EPDP. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Hundred Years' War: Charles VII of France is crowned the King of France in the Reims Cathedral after a successful campaign by Joan of Arc.
items.body: The Hundred Years' War was a conflict between the kingdoms of England and France and a civil war in France during the late Middle Ages. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.

items.hook: The Spanish expedition to Formosa was a campaign mounted by the Spanish based in Manila, Philippines in 1626.
items.body: It was the Spanish response to Dutch settlements being built in Formosa, now known as Taiwan. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: King Mohammed Zahir Shah of Afghanistan, while having surgery in Italy, is deposed by his cousin Mohammed Daoud Khan.
items.body: Mohammad Zahir Shah was the last king of Afghanistan, reigning from 8 November 1933 until he was deposed on 17 July 1973. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The Invite review – A-list ensemble electrify hilarious couples night gone wrong comedy
items.body:
Sundance film festival: Olivia Wilde, Seth Rogen, Penelope Cruz and Edward Norton are exceptional in a smart and funny winner about sex, marriage and partner-swapping

items.hook: Sikandar Lodi succeeds Bahlul Khan Lodi as Sultan of Delhi.
items.body: Sikandar Khan Lodi, born Nizam Khan also known as Sikandar II, was Sultan of the Delhi Sultanate between 1489 and 1517. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: World War II: The main three leaders of the Allied nations, Winston Churchill, Harry S. Truman and Joseph Stalin, hold the Potsdam Conference in the German city of Potsdam to decide the future of a defeated Germany.
items.body: The Allies, or Allied powers, formally referred to as the United Nations from 1942, were an international military coalition formed during World War II (1939–1945) to oppose the Axis powers. One of those things you can't un-know.
